subject: Geology
title: Building Materials
description: A short lesson to help you apply Building Materials.

hook.text: The Parthenon is marble. The pyramids are limestone. The roads beneath your tires are crushed granite.

buildup.text: Rocks have been humanity's primary building material since the Stone Age. We still depend on them today.

discovery.text: Granite resists weathering. Limestone is easy to carve. Slate makes waterproof roofs. Each rock has its strength.

twist.text: Concrete is artificial rock. It's a mix of cement (from limestone), sand, gravel, and water, mimicking nature.

climax.text: Modern cities are built from processed rock. Steel comes from iron ore; glass from sand; cement from limestone.

punchline.text: Civilization is built from shaped stone.

quiz.question: What is concrete primarily made from?
options: Cement from limestone, sand, gravel, and water
